start_cue: Pick a target first — elimination, explicit numeric cap, or a 30-to-90-day experiment. If you don't know which, run the experiment and let data decide.

weeks.focus: Cut drinks per occasion in half
weeks.detail: Same nights out, same social context — just half the drinks at each. Nothing else changes this week. The point is to establish that reduction is possible without removing the occasions themselves.

Pick a weekly drinking occasion and make it a non-drinking night. A Thursday is usually easiest. Friday and Saturday can stay at the week-one reduced count. For cannabis: apply a time-of-day restriction (no use before evening) or a context restriction (no use on training days).

A second occasion comes out. Down to one or two drinking occasions per week with drinks-per-occasion halved. Pre-commit rules for any scheduled events in the coming weeks: decide now what you'll do at each, not in the moment.

weeks.focus: Hold the reduced level and evaluate
weeks.detail: One to two occasions per week at halved count. Track drinks-per-week or days-of-use-per-week in a phone note. The tracking is for signal, not accountability — so you can honestly answer 'is this working' at 30 days.

graduation: Reassess at 30 and 90 days. Appearance changes show up on the sobriety timeline; performance changes show up in training logs and sleep. If the expected changes are showing, the plan is working — continue or tighten. If they are not, the intake target is probably still too high, not too low.
